Kemal Özkırşehirli / Projects

Projects

Research, engineering, writing, and experiments worth making public.

No projects match that filter.

PRISM AI Safety Fellow — Protein Foundation Model Red-Teaming - May 2026 – Present - Developing adversarial evaluation methods for protein foundation models, focusing on biologically plausible but unreliable outputs, sequence–structure–function constraints, uncertainty calibration, and safe design reliability.

AI Safety - Protein Design - Evaluation

CV

ChromoGen-Engine V2 — Conditional Diffusion for 3D Chromatin - May 2026 – Present - Building a conditional diffusion and evaluation pipeline connecting small-molecule perturbation data, single-cell 3D chromatin generation, and same-cell multi-omic regulatory readouts.

Diffusion Models - Genomics - Chromatin

CV

A*STAR V2M-Engine — Autoimmune Target Discovery - May 2026 – Present - Architecting a genetics-to-mechanism framework that maps autoimmune GWAS and fine-mapped variants onto regulatory programs, causal immune states, and targetable mechanisms.

Drug Discovery - Single-Cell - Genetics

CV

TBXT Small-Molecule Hackathon — Student Principal Investigator - April 2026 – Present - Leading an 11-person chordoma-focused computational hit-identification sprint with docking, GNINA, QSAR, Boltz-2, MMGBSA/FEP scaffolding, selectivity gates, and novelty/sourceability filters.

CADD - Docking - QSAR - Leadership

CV

ChemAgent-QSM — LLM and Statistical Mechanics/Theoretical Chemistry - Dec 2025 – Present - Developing an LLM-guided agentic framework that converts natural-language chemistry prompts into validated quantum-chemical and statistical-mechanics workflows using PySCF and baseline simulation methods.

LLMs - Quantum Chemistry - Statistical Mechanics

CV

EVEdesign — Protein Design Algorithm Engineering - April 2026 – Present - Contributing algorithmic and engineering components for uncertainty-aware candidate selection, sequence–structure–function objectives, and lab-in-the-loop biosequence-design workflows.

Protein Design - Uncertainty - Biosequence Models

CV

Pedal AI — LangGraph Agentic Chemistry Platform - Jul–Dec 2025 - Designed backend architecture for LangGraph AI agents and molecular prediction engines, using Python/FastAPI to support high-throughput chemistry workflows and inspectable retrosynthesis trees.

LangGraph - Backend - Retrosynthesis

CV

Deep Reinforcement Learning for Antibody-Antigen Interactions - Jan 2026 – Present - Co-led an AI@MIT/AIM Labs team developing a structure-informed cross-attention transformer and ESM-2 pipeline for antibody candidates, with PPO-based reinforcement learning against structural metrics.

Reinforcement Learning - Antibodies - Protein Modeling

CV

ML for Statistical Mechanics Simulations — Prof. A. Nihat Berker - Jan 2022 – Present - Building a PyTorch/GNN-augmented Kadanoff renormalization-group framework to identify fixed points and thermodynamic phase boundaries in spin systems.

Statistical Mechanics - GNNs - Physics

CV

Kupcinet-Getz Scholar — Computational Biochemical Diffusion - Jun–Aug 2025 - Developed PINN- and neural-ODE-enhanced stochastic simulation algorithms for nonequilibrium enzyme reaction/diffusion networks at the Weizmann Institute.

Biochemical Diffusion - PINNs - Neural ODEs

CV

Advanced Organic Synthesis — Buchwald Lab - Dec 2025 – May 2026 - Supported development of CuH-catalyzed asymmetric methylation and alkylation of vinyl boronate esters, using NMR and chiral-HPLC to connect catalyst structure and activity.

Organic Chemistry - Synthesis - Catalysis

CV

Computational Organic Chemistry Pipeline — DFT to kMC - 2023–2024 · Lumiere Research Inclusion Foundation - Automated multi-scale simulation pipeline linking quantum mechanical DFT calculations to stochastic kinetic Monte Carlo models, screening transition states and reaction trajectories for yield optimization.

DFT - kMC - Computational Chemistry

CV

MIT 6.1200[J] Teaching Fellow and Grader - Jan 2026 – Present - Supporting a 250+ student proof-intensive discrete mathematics / mathematics for computer science course through recitation, office hours, grading, and exams.

Teaching - Algorithms - Discrete Mathematics

CV
